name: Hotel Hue Preference by Time of Day (Wang & Li)
description: Combined web-survey template for Studies 2, 3B, and 4 from Wang & Li (Tourism Management, 2027). Bundled Appendix C stimuli are folder-scoped (study2 / study3b / study4). Study 1 (eye-tracking) is omitted. Scale items from Appendix D (mmc1).
author: Wang & Li

config.title: Hotel Booking Preference Survey (Wang & Li, 2027 — Studies 2, 3B, 4)
config.description: Based on: Wang, L., & Li, S. (2027). The temporal shift in consumer hue preferences in hotel booking. Tourism Management, 118, 105481. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tourman.2026.105481. This combined template covers Study 2 (Ctrip pairwise choice), Study 3B (Fliggy single photo; calibrated luminance/saturation), and Study 4 (Ctrip twin-room pairwise choice + chronotype). Study 1 is lab eye-tracking and is not included. Measurement items from Appendix D (supplementary mmc1). Stimuli from Appendix C Figs. S1–S3. In the paper, Stage 2 surveys were administered in designated morning/evening windows.

elements.name: overview_note
elements.html:
Wang & Li (2027) — combined Studies 2, 3B, and 4.

  • Study 2 (Fig. S1): business-trip scenario; choose Weihao (cool) vs Mingting (warm).
  • Study 3B (Fig. S2): Fliggy weekend scenario; one of four daytime/nighttime × cool/warm photos (not Study 3A Booking.com stimuli).
  • Study 4 (Fig. S3): trip-with-friends scenario; choose Anyue (cool) vs Arton (warm); then chronotype (rMEQ).
  • Study 1 (eye-tracking) is omitted.

Need for uniqueness / belonging are asked once (Study 2 order); Study 4 in the paper re-measured them after choice.

pages.name: emotional_states
pages.title: Current emotional states
pages.description: Study 2 Stage 2 began with current emotional states (Appendix D; Singh, 2006). Study 3A/3B also measured current emotional state as a control.

elements.name: current_emotional_states
elements.title: To what extent do you feel happy/depressed?
elements.description: 1 = not at all, 7 = very much (Appendix D).

pages.name: study2_choice
pages.title: Study 2 — Hotel preference
pages.description: Study 2 scenario and DV (verbatim from the article). Stimuli: Appendix C Fig. S1 (folder study2).

elements.name: study2_scenario
elements.title: Please imagine the following:
elements.description: You have an upcoming business trip to conduct on-site visits and work with a partner company. For convenience, you wish to stay at a hotel within walking distance of their office. You open a booking platform and find two suitable hotels, both located within 500 m of the company.

elements.name: study2_hotel_preference
elements.title: If you were to book a room now, which hotel would you choose?
elements.description: Study 2 DV (binary). Fixed set: Weihao (cool) vs Mingting (warm), Fig. S1. mediaAssignmentMode=set so both photos are always shown together; left/right was counterbalanced in the article.

pages.name: need_for_uniqueness
pages.title: Need for uniqueness
pages.description: Ruvio et al. (2008); 1 = strongly disagree, 7 = strongly agree (Appendix D). Measured in Studies 2 and 4.

elements.name: need_for_uniqueness
elements.title: Need for uniqueness

rows.text: I actively seek to develop my personal uniqueness by buying special products.
The more commonplace a product is among the general population, the less interested I am in buying it.
I am often on the lookout for new products that will add to my personal uniqueness.

pages.name: need_for_belonging
pages.title: Need for belonging
pages.description: Leary et al. (2013); 1 = strongly disagree, 7 = strongly agree (Appendix D). Measured in Studies 2 and 4.

elements.name: need_for_belonging
elements.title: Need for belonging

rows.text: I try hard not to do things that will make other people avoid or reject me.
I do not like being alone.
I need to feel that there are people I can turn to in times of need.

pages.name: need_for_arousal
pages.title: Need for arousal
pages.description: Chen, Sun et al. (2023); three items (Appendix D). Study 2 alternative mediator.

elements.name: arousal_1
elements.title: How much arousal do you need?

elements.name: arousal_2
elements.title: I really want to cheer up.

elements.name: arousal_3
elements.title: I currently feel full of energy.

pages.name: self_control
pages.title: Self-control
pages.description: Tangney et al. (2004); 1 = strongly disagree, 7 = strongly agree (Appendix D). Study 2 alternative mediator.

elements.name: self_control
elements.title: Self-control

rows.text: I am good at resisting temptation and completing plans on time.
Even when there are more interesting things, I can still focus on important work/study.
I do not make decisions on impulse.

pages.name: study3b_stimulus
pages.title: Study 3B — Hotel evaluation
pages.description: Study 3B scenario (verbatim). Stimuli: Appendix C Fig. S2 (folder study3b); one of four conditions at random. Study 3A used Booking.com / Fig. 5 and is not included.

elements.name: study3b_scenario
elements.title: Please imagine the following:
elements.description: Please imagine you are planning a weekend getaway with a friend and intend to book a hotel in advance via Fliggy. Upon opening the platform, the following hotel is recommended to you on the homepage.

elements.name: study3b_hotel_photo
elements.title: Please view the recommended hotel photo.
elements.description: Study 3B: randomly one of four daytime/nighttime × cool/warm photos (Fig. S2). individual mode — not a fixed set.

elements.name: study3b_open_evaluation
elements.title: Please provide an overall evaluation of the hotel room.
elements.description: Study 3B open-ended question (minimum 30 characters), as stated in the article.

elements.name: study3b_booking_likelihood
elements.title: How likely are you to book this hotel?
elements.description: Study 3A/3B DV (Chen, Ahlstrom, & Xiao, 2023): likelihood of booking the hotel; 1 = not at all likely, 7 = very likely. Study 3B used the same questionnaire as Study 3A.

elements.name: study3b_window_view_check
elements.title: How would you describe the scene outside the window?

elements.name: study3b_hue_check
elements.title: Please rate the color hue of this hotel photo.
elements.description: Hue manipulation check (Study 3A/3B). Exact stem not quoted in the article; 7-point cool–warm scale consistent with reported condition means (higher = warmer).

pages.name: study4_choice
pages.title: Study 4 — Hotel preference
pages.description: Study 4 scenario and DV (verbatim). Stimuli: Appendix C Fig. S3 (folder study4). Twin rooms for companion-based travel.

elements.name: study4_scenario
elements.title: Please imagine the following:
elements.description: Please imagine you are planning a trip with friends and searching for a hotel at your destination using the Ctrip application. You have discovered two hotels with similar overall conditions.

elements.name: study4_hotel_preference
elements.title: If you were to book a room now for a trip, which hotel would you choose?
elements.description: Study 4 DV (binary). Fixed set: Anyue (cool) vs Arton (warm), Fig. S3. mediaAssignmentMode=set so both photos are always shown together.

pages.name: chronotype
pages.title: Chronotype
pages.description: Reduced Morningness–Eveningness items (Smith et al., 1989; Wu et al., 2025). Study 4 moderator; listed in Appendix D.

elements.name: chronotype_1
elements.title: Generally speaking, do you consider yourself more of a ‘morning person’ or an ‘evening person’?
elements.description: 1 = definitely a morning type (morning alert and evening tired), 7 = definitely an evening type (morning tired and evening alert) (Appendix D).

elements.name: chronotype_2
elements.title: At what time of day do you usually feel most energetic and mentally alert?

elements.name: chronotype_3
elements.title: At what time in the evening do you typically feel tired and in need of sleep?

elements.name: education
elements.title: What is your highest level of educational attainment?

elements.name: monthly_income
elements.title: What is your monthly income?

elements.name: economic_social_class
elements.title: Compared to your peers, how would you rate your economic status and social class?

elements.name: booking_frequency
elements.title: On average, how many times per year do you book a hotel?

elements.name: outdoor_temperature
elements.title: What is the current outdoor temperature?

elements.name: indoor_temperature
elements.title: What is the current indoor temperature?

elements.name: weather
elements.title: What is the current outdoor weather?
elements.description: Weather Conditions (Motoki et al., 2019); Appendix D.

pages.name: demographics
pages.title: Demographics
pages.description: Gender and age as reported in Study demographic tables (Appendix G).

elements.name: gender
elements.title: Gender

elements.name: age
elements.title: Age
